name: gardener-cicd-cli
documentation: Gardener CI/CD Command Line Interface

functions.name: gardener_ci.checkmarx_cli.generate_scan_archive
documentation:
create checkmarx scan archive with filters applied
does not upload to scan API

commit_hash defaults to repo main branch

modules.name: gardener_ci.productutil_v2
documentation:
temporary (hopefully short..) migration util as a backwards-compatible replacement for
productutil.py -> add_dependencies

different to the aforementioned cmd, an ocm-component-descriptor is expected as input, and
created as output

modules.name: gardener_ci.compliance
documentation:
a collection of hopefully useful tools for compliance-related, half-automated tasks

since some commands will require many (and lengthy) arguments, which, in many cases, will
rarely change, callers may optionally specify a "defaults file" (which is expected to be a YAML
document containing default-values using CLI-arg-names).

Example defaults-file contents:

```
left_name: github.com/example/example
left_version: 1.2.3
right_name: github.com/example/example
right_version: 2.0.0
ocm_repo_urls:
 - eu.gcr.io/example/example-repo
```

modules.name: gardener_ci.githubutil
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company and Gardener contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: gardener_ci.githubutil.list_draft_releases
documentation:
List all draft releases in a GitHub repository. If the `--only-outdated` flag is set,
only outdated draft releases are printed. A draft release is considered outdated iff:
    1: its version is smaller than the greatest release version (according to semver) AND
        2a: it is NOT a hotfix draft release AND
        2b: there are no hotfix draft releases with the same major and minor version
        OR
        3a: it is a hotfix draft release AND
        3b: there is a hotfix draft release of greater version (according to semver)
            with the same major and minor version

Hotfix draft release in this context are draft releases with a semver patch version that is
not equal to 0.

functions.name: gardener_ci.githubutil.greatest_release_version
documentation:
Find the release with the greatest name (according to semver) and print its semver-version.

Note:
- This will only consider releases whose names are either immediately parseable as semver-
versions, or prefixed with a single character ('v').
- The 'v'-prefix (if present) will be not be present in the output.
- If a release has no name, its tag will be used instead of its name.

For more details on the ordering of semantic versioning, see 'https://www.semver.org'.

functions.name: gardener_ci.gh.auth_user_and_token
documentation: write privileged user credentials for repo in format username:password to stdout

functions.name: gardener_ci.pipeline._branch_cfg
documentation: parses `branch.cfg` from repo, assuming it is a valid yaml-file named `branch.cfg`

functions.name: gardener_ci.pipeline._pipeline_definitions

modules.name: gardener_ci.cli_gen
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company and Gardener contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: gardener_ci.cli_gen._subcommand_modules
documentation:
returns a dict where keys are subcommand-names, and values are module-names
(module-names are sometimes prefixed with an underscore in this directory to avoid
name conflicts)

functions.name: gardener_ci.cli_gen.main
documentation:
Creates a command line parser (using argparse) for each python module found in this
directory (except for _this_ module). For each module, a sub-command named as the
module name is added. Each function defined in a given module is again added as a
sub-sub-command. Based on the function signature, optional arguments are added.
This parser is then used to parse the given ARGV. Provided that parsing succeeds,
the thus specified function is executed.

functions.name: gardener_ci._cfg_mgmt.process_config_queue
documentation:
Iterates to be deleted cfg_queue_entries with optional cfg_target filter.
Processes very first supported entry and terminates.

functions.name: gardener_ci._ocm.traverse
documentation:
name: either component-name, or <component-name>:<version>
version: optional, if not passed w/ name (no value-checking will be done!)
components: whether to print components
sources: whether to print sources
resources: whether to print resources
print_expr: python-expression (passed to `eval()` w/ globals: {'node': node})
